steps.title: Backsolving — testing choice B (-0.8)
steps.content:
Read two points that lie on the line of best fit from the scatterplot: the line passes through (0, 2) and (5, -2).
Compute slope: $$m = \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1} = \frac{-2 - 2}{5 - 0} = \frac{-4}{5} = -0.8.$$
This slope matches choice B, and the line with this slope fits the general trend of the data (points lie close to it).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
